questions.title: The fundamental right of fair trial and due process under Article 10-A of the Constitution 1973 is available at which stage of the proceedings?
questions.explanation:
The fundamental right of fair trial and due process under Article 10-A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 is available at all stages of proceedings, including:

  • Lodging of complaint,
  • Service of summons,
  • Framing of charge and institution of suit,
  • Trial stage,
  • Appellate stage,
  • And any other stage where the rights of an individual are determined by a court or tribunal.

The first Battle of Tarain was fought in 1191 between the invading Ghurid army, led by Muhammad of Ghor, and the Rajput Confederacy led by Prithviraj Chauhan.

The battle took place near Tarain (modern-day Taraori) in Haryana, India.

questions.title: Amir Khusaru was a famous court poet of _____?
questions.explanation:
Amir Khusrau was a prolific classical poet associated with the royal courts of emperors.

He was a Sufi musician, poet and scholar from India around the 13th century.

He is also called “father of qawwali” and is credited with the creation of qawwali form.

He became a disciple of Sufi Saint Nizamuddin Auliya.

He also brought into circulation the two specific musical genres of 'Tarana' and 'Kaul', which complemented the prevalent array of musical forms.

Alaudin khilji appreciated poetry and invited many poets to his courts but khusro was honoured and respected in his court and given the title” Amir”.

The famous poet Amir Khusrau composed poetry in Urdu.

questions.title: Timur, the Lame invaded India during the reign of _____?
questions.explanation:
Timur (Tamerlane) invaded India in 1398 during the reign of Sultan Nasir-ud-Din Muhammad bin Tughlaq.

His invasion led to massive destruction in Delhi and weakened the Delhi Sultanate significantly.

questions.title: Which of the following was the last dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation:
The Lodhi Dynasty was the last dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

It was ruling from 1451 until the defeat by Babur in 1526, which led to the establishment of the Mughal Empire.

The first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate was the Mamluk Dynasty (also known as the Slave Dynasty), which began with Qutb al-Din Aibak in 1206.

questions.title: Padshah was the title of which Mughal King?
questions.explanation:
Padshah:

It means emperor, and Babur was the first Mughal emperor to use this title.

Babur:

He was a descendant of both Timur and Genghis Khan, and he founded the Mughal Empire in India in 1526.

Additional Information

Jahangir:

He was Babur's grandson and the fourth Mughal emperor.

Humayun:

He was Babur's son and the second Mughal emperor.

Akbar:

He was Babur's grandson and the third Mughal emperor.

questions.title: Field Marshal Asim Munir was awarded which of the following on 79th impendence day of Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
On 14th August 2025, President Asif Ali Zardari awarded Hilal-e-Jurat to Field Marshal Asim Munir and Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Babar Sidhu.

Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee General Sahir Shamshad Mirza and Chief of Naval Staff Admiral Naveed Ashraf were awarded the Nishan-e-Imtiaz (Military).

Awarded for their exceptional contributions during Indo Pak war 2025.

questions.title: During the reign of Alauddin Khilji, who were Amil?
questions.answer: Government agents to collect land revenue
questions.explanation:
During Alauddin Khilji’s reign, Amils were appointed as government officials responsible for collecting land revenue.

They were key agents in the administration of the Iqta system, ensuring efficient revenue collection from villages.

questions.title: Who among the following Delhi Sultans is known for introducing market control mechanism?
questions.explanation:
Alauddin Khilji (1296–1316) is known for implementing market control measures in Delhi to stabilize prices and ensure supply for his army.

He introduced price control, rationing, and strict regulation of trade, along with monitoring grain and commodity markets to prevent hoarding and profiteering.

questions.title: When German Foreign Minister Annalena Baerbock visited Pakistan for the first time?
questions.explanation:
At the invitation of Foreign Minister Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Annalena Baerbock, the Federal Minister for Foreign Affairs of the Federal Republic of Germany, visited Pakistan on June 7-8, 2022.

This was Foreign Minister Baerbock's first visit to Pakistan.

questions.title: The Presiding head of National Assembly _________?
questions.explanation:
The Speaker is the presiding head of the National Assembly of Pakistan.
The Speaker conducts proceedings, maintains order, and represents the Assembly in its relations with the President and other authorities.

questions.title: The expenditure of the National Assembly and Senate of Pakistan within authorized appropriation shall be controlled by the National Assembly or, in the case may be, the Senate on the advice of _____?
questions.explanation:
The expenditure of the National Assembly and Senate of Pakistan within authorized appropriation shall be controlled by the National Assembly or, as the case may be, the Senate, on the advice of its Finance Committee.

This is stated in Article 88 of the Constitution of Pakistan.

questions.title: The 2nd Constitution (1962) of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan begin with _____?
questions.explanation:
The Constitution of 1962 of Pakistan contained a Preamble.
The Preamble was based on the Objectives Resolution of 1949, forming its guiding principles.

questions.title: Protection of property rights is a _____ in Article 24 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.
questions.explanation:
Article 24 of the 1973 Constitution protects citizens from deprivation of property except by law.

It is categorized under Fundamental Rights in Part II, Chapter 1 of the Constitution.

questions.title: What was the structure of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan in the first Constitution of Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
The 1956 Constitution declared Pakistan as the Islamic Republic of Pakistan with a Federal structure.

It had a unicameral legislature (National Assembly), with representation based on parity between East and West Pakistan.

questions.title: How many constituent assemblies were made to frame the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
First Constituent Assembly (1947–1954): Tasked with framing the constitution but dissolved before completion.

Second Constituent Assembly (1955–1956): Completed the work of the first and passed the 1956 Constitution.

Third Constituent Assembly (1972–1973): Drafted and passed the 1973 Constitution, which is still in force (with amendments).

questions.title: Which great problem became a hurdle in the way of formation of the first Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
The Urdu-Bengali language controversy created serious conflict between East and West Pakistan after independence.

This dispute delayed consensus in the Constituent Assembly, becoming a major hurdle in framing the first Constitution (1956).

questions.title: As per Article 176 of Constitution of Pakistan 1973, The Supreme Court shall consist of a Chief Justice Shall be known as chief justice of Pakistan and so many other judges as may be determined by act of Majlis-e-Shoora or untill so determined as may be fixed by the _______ of Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
The President is the head of state of Pakistan.

Article 176 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 states that the number of Supreme Court judges, until fixed by Act of Majlis-e-Shoora, shall be determined by the President of Pakistan.

The Chief Justice of Pakistan is appointed separately, while the President decides the total strength of judges until Parliament legislates otherwise.

questions.title: As per Article 75(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, when the ______ has assented or is deemed to have assented to a Bill, it shall become law and be called an Act of Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament).
questions.explanation:
Article 75(3) states that when the President assents or is deemed to have assented to a Bill, it becomes law.

After assent, it is formally called an Act of Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament).

questions.title: As per Article 40 of the Constitution of Pakistan, the State shall endeavor to ______ based on Islamic unity, support the common interests of the People of Asia, Africa and Latin America, promote International Peace and Security, foster among all nations and encourage the settlement of international disputes by peaceful means.
questions.answer: Preserve and strengthen fraternal relations among Muslim countries
questions.explanation:
Article 40 directs Pakistan’s foreign policy to preserve and strengthen fraternal ties with Muslim countries, reflecting Islamic solidarity.

It also emphasizes cooperation with Asia, Africa, and Latin America, while promoting peaceful international dispute resolution.

questions.title: As per Article 53(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, after a general election the National Assembly shall _____ elect from amongst is members of Speaker and Deputy Speaker and so after the office of Speaker becomes vacant, the Assembly shall elect another member as Speaker, or as the case may be Deputy Speaker.
questions.answer: At its first meeting and to the exclusion of any other business
questions.explanation:
Article 53(1) states that the National Assembly must elect its Speaker and Deputy Speaker at its very first meeting, before taking up anything else.

This ensures that the Assembly has proper presiding officers to conduct further proceedings in a lawful manner.

questions.title: As per Article 32 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the State shall encourage local Government institutions composed of elected representative of the areas concerned and in such institutions, special representation will be given to _____?
questions.explanation:
Article 32 directs the State to promote local government institutions with elected representatives.

It further ensures special representation for peasants, workers, and women in these bodies.

questions.title: As per Article 36 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the State shall safeguard the legitimate rights and interests of minorities, including their due representation in ________?
questions.explanation:
Article 36 of the Constitution ensures protection of minorities’ rights.

It specifically mentions their due representation in the Federal and Provincial services.

questions.title: As per the Preamble of the Constitution, whereas sovereignty over the entire universe belongs to Almighty Allah alone, and the authority to be exercised by the people of Pakistan within the limits prescribed by him is a sacred _____?
questions.explanation:
The Preamble of the Constitution of Pakistan declares that sovereignty belongs to Almighty Allah alone.

The authority given to the people of Pakistan is a sacred trust, to be exercised within the limits prescribed by Him.

questions.title: According to Article of the Constitution of Pakistan, steps shall be taken to enable the Muslims of Pakistan, individually and collectively to order their lives under the fundamental principles and basic facilities whereby they may be enabled to _____?
questions.answer: Understand the meaning of life according to the Holy Quran and Sunnah
questions.explanation:
As per Article 31 of the Constitution of Pakistan (Islamic way of life), the State must enable Muslims to order their lives in accordance with the Qur’an and Sunnah.

It ensures steps for Islamic education, moral values, and Shariah compliance in personal and collective life.

questions.title: Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff (PLD 1977 S.C. 657) Case, Begum Nusrat Bhutto challenged the detention of her husband and ten other leaders of Pakistan People's Party in the Supreme Court of Pakistan by filling a Constitutional petition under Article _____?
questions.explanation:
In Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff (PLD 1977 SC 657), she filed a petition under Article 184(3) of the Constitution.

This Article empowers the Supreme Court to enforce Fundamental Rights in matters of public importance.

questions.title: As per the case of Zafar Ali Shah and others vs General Pervez Musharraf, Chief Executive of Pakistan (PLD 2000 S.C 869), the Bench was presided by _____?
questions.explanation:
• Bench Composition: The case was heard by a 12-member bench.
  • Presiding Judge: Irshad Hasan Khan (Chief Justice).
  • Outcome: The court validated the 1999 military coup under the doctrine of state necessity and granted the regime a three-year period

Last updated on NS-16-April-2026

questions.title: As per the Case of Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), the Supreme Court held that _____ it had no jurisdiction.
questions.explanation:
In Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 SC 139), the Supreme Court declared General Yahya Khan a usurper and held Martial Law as unconstitutional and illegal.

The Court overruled Dosso’s case and rejected Kelsen’s theory of revolutionary legality.

questions.title: In the State vs Dosso Case, the Supreme Court under justice Munir upheld the Martial Law relying upon _____?
questions.answer: Hans Kelsen's General Theory of Law and State
questions.explanation:
In State vs. Dosso (PLD 1958 SC 533), Chief Justice Munir applied Kelsen’s theory of revolutionary legality, which validated a successful coup as creating a new legal order.

This judgment legitimized the first Martial Law in Pakistan (1958) and set a precedent for future interventions.

questions.title: In the case of Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), by majority it was held that the order of April 1993, passed by the President of Pakistan is ______?
questions.answer: Not within powers conferred on the President under Article 52(2)(b) of the Constitution
questions.explanation:
In PLD 1993 SC 473 (Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan), the Supreme Court held that the President’s dissolution of the National Assembly was beyond his constitutional powers.

The Assembly was consequently restored, reaffirming the limits on the President’s authority under Article 58(2)(b).

questions.title: The Supreme Court of Pakistan have the power to make an order of nature mentioned in Article 199 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, if it considers that there is a question of public importance with reference to enforcement of ______?
questions.explanation:
Article 199 of the Constitution 1973 empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs for enforcement of fundamental rights.

Such orders ensure protection of citizens’ fundamental rights against illegal actions by the state or public authorities.

questions.title: As per Article 186 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the President can seek the advice of Supreme Court on any _____ which considers of public importance.
questions.explanation:
Article 186 of the Constitution 1973 allows the President to seek the advice of the Supreme Court on questions of law of public importance.

This ensures that legal clarity and guidance are provided before executive action.

questions.title: As per Article 178 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, before entering upon office, the Chief Justice of Pakistan shall make before the President and any other Judge of the Supreme Court shall make before the Chief Justice, oath in the form set out in the ______ Schedule.
questions.explanation:
As per Article 178 of the Constitution of Pakistan, the oath for the Chief Justice of Pakistan and other Supreme Court Judges is set out in the Third Schedule.

This schedule, which also includes oaths for other offices, specifies the exact wording of the oath that must be taken before entering office, as stated in the Constitution itself.

questions.title: As per Article 55(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973,, the minimum Quorum for session of National Assembly is _____ of the members.
questions.explanation:
Article 55(2) of the Constitution 1973 provides that the quorum of the National Assembly is one-fourth of its total membership.

If quorum is not met, the House must be adjourned or suspended until it is complete.

questions.title: According to Zafar Ali Shah Case (PLD 200 S.C 869), the abuse of public officer for private gain is _____?
questions.explanation:
In Zafar Ali Shah case (PLD 2000 SC 869), the Court described the abuse of public office for private gain as corruption.

It emphasized that corruption undermines public trust and good governance.

questions.title: In the case of Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), the petitioner challenged the order of dissolution of National Assembly passed under Article 58(2)(b) of the Constitution of Pakistan by the ______?
questions.explanation:
In Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs. President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 SC 473), Nawaz Sharif challenged the dissolution of the National Assembly by the President under Article 58(2)(b).

The Supreme Court restored the Assembly and Nawaz Sharif’s government, declaring the dissolution unconstitutional.

questions.title: As per Article 60(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the term of office of the Chairman or Deputy Chairman shall be _____ years from the day on which he enters upon his office.
questions.explanation:
Article 60(2) specifies that the Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Senate hold office for three years.

This term limit ensures periodic leadership rotation in the Senate.

questions.title: As per Article 10(2) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973, every person who is arrested and detained in custody shall be produced before a ______ in twenty four hours of such arrest.
questions.explanation:
Article 10(2) of the Constitution 1973 states that an arrested person must be produced before a Magistrate within 24 hours.

This safeguards against illegal detention and ensures judicial oversight of custody.

questions.title: Article of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973, envisaged that to enjoy the protection of the law and to be treated in accordance with law is the ______ right of every citizen of Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Article 4 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 provides that to enjoy protection of law and to be treated in accordance with law is the inalienable right of every citizen.

This ensures that no citizen can be deprived of legal protection except in accordance with law.

questions.title: As per case "The State vs Dosso and Other (PLD 1958 S.C 533)" legalized _____?
questions.explanation:
In State vs. Dosso (PLD 1958 SC 533), the Supreme Court applied Hans Kelsen’s theory of revolutionary legality and validated Martial Law imposed by General Ayub Khan.

This judgment gave legal cover to unconstitutional takeovers, later criticized in constitutional history.

questions.title: The Vires of National Accountability Ordinance 1999 were challenged in ______?
questions.answer: Asfand Yar Wali and Others vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 SC 607)
questions.explanation:
The Vires (legal validity) of the National Accountability Ordinance (NAO) 1999 were challenged in the Supreme Court of Pakistan.

After the military takeover in 1999, numerous petitions were filed challenging the Ordinance's validity under Article 184(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan, and the Supreme Court dealt with these challenges.

questions.title: As per Article 206 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a judge of the Supreme Courts may resign his office by writing under his hand addressed to the _____?
questions.explanation:
Article 206 states that a judge of the Supreme Court may resign by writing under his hand addressed to the President.

The President is the appointing authority, hence resignation is formally submitted to him.

questions.title: According to Article 60 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the term of office of the Chairman or Deputy Chairman Senate shall be _____ years from the day on which he enters upon his office.
questions.explanation:
Article 60 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 specifies that the Chairman and Deputy Chairman Senate hold office for 3 years.

This ensures rotation of leadership within the Senate for balanced representation.

questions.title: As per Article 76(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a bill pending in the Senate which has not been passed by the National Assembly shall not laps on the dissolution of the _____?
questions.explanation:
Article 76(2) states that if a bill is pending in the Senate, it does not lapse upon the dissolution of the National Assembly.

This ensures continuity of legislative business in the Senate despite the Assembly’s dissolution.

questions.title: Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff (PLD 1977 S.C. 657) Case, the Chief Martial Law administrator was justified in providing in Article 2(3) of Law (Continuous in Force) Order 1977 that right to enforce _____?
questions.explanation:
In Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff (PLD 1977 SC 657), the Supreme Court upheld Article 2(3) of the Law (Continuous in Force) Order 1977, allowing suspension of fundamental rights during martial law.

This provision temporarily restricted citizens’ constitutional rights in favor of martial law administration.

questions.title: As per Article 3 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the _____ shall ensure the elimination of all forms of exploitation and the gradual fulfilment of the fundamental principle, from each according to his ability to each according to his work.
questions.explanation:
Article 3 places the responsibility on the State to eliminate exploitation.

It also promotes the principle of fair contribution and reward based on ability and work.

questions.title: According to Article 52 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1974, the National Assembly shall, unless sooner dissolved, continue for a term of five years from the day of _____ and shall stand dissolved at the expiration of its term.
questions.explanation:
Article 52 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 provides that the National Assembly shall continue for five years from the day of its first meeting.

After completion of this term, it automatically stands dissolved, unless dissolved earlier.

questions.title: As per case "Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1996 S.C. 324"), the case was filed in Supreme Court in appeal from the judgment dated 4-9-1994 of the Lahore High Court, in writ petition no. 875 of 1884 is about appointment of ______?
questions.answer: Judges of Supreme Court and High Court
questions.explanation:
The Al-Jehad Trust Case (PLD 1996 SC 324), also known as the Judges’ Case, dealt with the procedure of appointment of Judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts.

It established judicial independence by emphasizing that the opinion of the Chief Justice of Pakistan holds primacy in such appointments.

questions.explanation:
Nanga Parbat is known as the "Killer Mountain" due to unpredictable weather, steep inclines, and a high number of climbing fatalities.

It is one of the deadliest mountains in the world for mountaineers.

Nanga Parbat, locally known as Diamer, is the ninth highest mountain in the world at 8,126 metres above sea level.

questions.title: In the case of Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), who was the Chief Justice?
questions.explanation:
In the case of Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), Nasim Hassan Shah was the Chief Justice.

He headed the Supreme Court bench that delivered the judgment in this significant constitutional case.

questions.title: Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff (PLD 1977 S.C. 657), when this case was decided who was the Chief Justice?
questions.explanation:
The case was decided in 1977 after the imposition of martial law by General Zia-ul-Haq.

Chief Justice Sheikh Anwar-ul-Haq presided over the Supreme Court at that time.

questions.title: In Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C 240) case, the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was dissolved by _____ on October 24, 1954.
questions.explanation:
On 24 October 1954, Governor General Malik Ghulam Muhammad dissolved Pakistan’s first Constituent Assembly.

The decision was later upheld by Chief Justice Muhammad Munir in the Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din case (PLD 1955 FC 240).

questions.title: As per Article 20(b) of Constitution of Pakistan 1973, Subject to law, public order and morality every religious denomination and every sect thereof _____ have the right to establish, maintain and manage its religious institutions.
questions.explanation:
Article 20(b) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 provides that every religious denomination and sect thereof shall have the right to establish, maintain and manage its religious institutions.

This guarantees freedom of religion subject to law, public order, and morality.

questions.title: As per Article 175(2) of Constitution of Pakistan 1973, No Court shall have any jurisdiction save aa is or may be conferred on it by the the Constitution or by or under _____ law.
questions.explanation:
Article 175(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 provides that No Court shall have any jurisdiction save as is or may be conferred on it by the Constitution or by or under any law.

This ensures courts exercise jurisdiction only when authorized by law.

questions.title: As per the Case of Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), the precise question the Supreme Court was whether the High Courts had jurisdiction under Article 98 of the _____ to enquire into the validity of detention under the Martial Law Regulation No. 78 of 1971 in view of the bar created by the provisions of jurisdiction of Courts (Removal of Doubts) Order 1969.
questions.explanation:
The Asma Jillani case was decided when the 1962 Constitution was in force.

Article 98 of the 1962 Constitution dealt with the High Courts’ writ jurisdiction.

questions.title: In the case of Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S C 473), it was held the dismissal of the Prime Minister along with his Cabinet and the dissolution of the National Assembly under the purported exercise of power conferred on the President under Article 58(2)(b) could be upheld. This unlawful action moreover was also violative of _____?
questions.explanation:
In PLD 1993 SC 473 (Nawaz Sharif vs. President of Pakistan), the Court held that dissolving the National Assembly under Article 58(2)(b) was unlawful.

It also violated the Fundamental Rights guaranteed by the Constitution.

questions.title: As per Article 4(1) of Constitution of Pakistan 1973, to enjoy the protection of law and to be treated in accordance with law is the ______ right of every citizen. Wherever he may be, and of every other person for the time being within Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Article 4(1) guarantees that protection and treatment in accordance with law is the inalienable right of every citizen.

This right also extends to any person within Pakistan, not just citizens.

questions.title: As per Article 7 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, in this part, unless the context otherwise requires, The State, means the _____ Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament) a Provincial Government, a Provincial Assembly, and such local or other authorities in Pakistan as are by law empowered to impose any tax or cess.
questions.explanation:
Article 7 defines “The State” to include the Federal Government, Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament), Provincial Governments, Provincial Assemblies, and local authorities empowered by law to levy taxes.

This broad definition ensures all governing tiers are covered.

questions.title: The Case of State vs Dosso and other PLD 1958, S.C 533, the Supreme Court decided unanimously against the verdict of ____?
questions.explanation:
In the case of State vs Dosso (PLD 1958, S.C. 533), the Supreme Court decided unanimously against the verdict of the Lahore High Court.

The Supreme Court's decision, which validated the first Martial Law in Pakistan, reversed the High Court's decision.

questions.title: Who was the Sikh ruler who fought against the British in the Anglo-Sikh Wars?
questions.explanation:
Maharaja Ranjit Singh (the “Lion of Punjab”) built the Sikh Empire, but he died in 1839 — before the Anglo-Sikh Wars (1845–1849)

. The wars were fought during the reign of his son, Maharaja Dalip Singh,

questions.title: As per Article 63-A (5) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, any party aggrieved by the decision of the Election Commission may, within thirty days, prefer an appeal to the Supreme Court which shall decide the matter within _____ from the date of filling of the appeal.
questions.explanation:
According to Article 63-A(5) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, any party aggrieved by a decision of the Election Commission may, within thirty days, appeal to the Supreme Court.

The Supreme Court is mandated to decide the matter within ninety days from the date the appeal is filed.

questions.title: Who was the Pakistani leader who played a key role in the Bangladesh Liberation War (1971)?
questions.explanation:

—under the orders of Yahya Khan

—launched Operation Searchlight against East Pakistanis on the night of 25 March 1971

questions.title: As per Article 211 of Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the proceedings before the Council, it's report to the President and the removal of. Judge under clause (6) of Article 209 shall not be called in question in _____?
questions.explanation:
Article 211 bars judicial review of proceedings of the Supreme Judicial Council regarding judges’ removal.

Neither the High Court nor Supreme Court can question such proceedings.

questions.title: Under Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Chairman and Deputy Chairman of Senate to take oaths before entering upon their offices in the form set out in the ______?
questions.explanation:
The oath of office for the Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Senate is prescribed in the Third Schedule of the 1973 Constitution.

This Schedule contains oath formats for key constitutional offices, ensuring loyalty to Pakistan and adherence to the Constitution.

questions.title: As per Article 75(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, when a Bill is presented to the President shall, within _____ days assent to the Bill or returned the bill to Parliament.
questions.explanation:
Article 75(1) states the President has 10 days to either assent to a Bill or return it with observations.

If not returned within this time, the Bill is deemed assented.

questions.title: English was declared as the official language of Pakistan in:
questions.explanation:
Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution
English was declared as the official language of Pakistan in the 1962 constitution.

questions.explanation:
Dr Abdul Qadeer Khan is buried in Islamabad.

questions.title: What is the name of the famous poet who wrote "Tulip-e-Haisal"?
questions.explanation:
The poet who wrote "Tulip-e-Haisal" was Faiz Ahmad Faiz

Genre: Poetry (Urdu and Punjabi) 

Significance: Faiz is renowned for his work that spoke to the struggles of the common people and his collection of poems, which have become a part of the national psyche.

questions.title: Which desert is located in Eastern part of Punjab?
questions.explanation:
Thal Desert and Cholistan Desert are located in Punjab province of Pakistan.

Cholistan is located in southern while Thal is located in Eastern part of Punjab.

questions.title: Pakistan celebrated independence day on 14 August 2021?
questions.explanation:
Pakistan celebrated 74th Independence Day with patriotic zeal and fervour on 14 August 2021.

Pakistan came into being on 14 August 1947.
